title: COBIT APO13.01 — ISMS establishment: security-policy compliance and control-effectiveness monitoring
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=COBIT, clause=APO13.01, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-C authored UC targeting COBIT APO13.01 (Manage security — establish and maintain an ISMS). Monitors security policy compliance status across control domains; assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the regulation-coverage plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that COBIT APO13.01 (Manage security — establish and maintain an ISMS) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.26: COBIT APO13.01 — ISMS establishment: security-policy compliance and control-effectiveness monitoring.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.26' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Security policy audit logs, GRC platform exports, ES notable events, configuration-compliance scans.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
spl:
index=grc_compliance sourcetype IN (policy_compliance, control_assessment) earliest=-30d
| eval control_effective=if(status IN ("pass", "compliant", "effective"), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_controls sum(control_effective) AS effective_controls dc(control_domain) AS domains_assessed values(eval(if(status NOT IN ("pass", "compliant", "effective"), control_id, null()))) AS failing_controls BY control_domain
| eval effectiveness_pct=round(100*effective_controls/total_controls, 1)
| where effectiveness_pct<100
| sort effectiveness_pct
| table control_domain, total_controls, effective_controls, effectiveness_pct, failing_controls
description: Aggregates security-policy compliance and control-assessment results across ISMS control domains to provide evidence of APO13.01 ISMS establishment and maintenance.
value: APO13.01 requires establishing and maintaining an ISMS aligned with enterprise strategy. Continuous compliance monitoring across control domains provides auditable evidence of ISMS effectiveness.
implementation: (1) Ingest GRC/policy-compliance scan results with control_id, control_domain, and status; (2) schedule weekly; (3) escalate domains below 100% effectiveness to CISO dashboard.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: APO13.01 requires maintaining a security management system — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Security policy audit logs, GRC platform exports, ES notable events, configuration-compliance scans.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest GRC/policy-compliance scan results with control_id, control_domain, and status; (2) schedule weekly; (3) escalate domains below 100% effectiveness to CISO dashboard.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc_compliance sourcetype IN (policy_compliance, control_assessment) earliest=-30d
| eval control_effective=if(status IN ("pass", "compliant", "effective"), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_controls sum(control_effective) AS effective_controls dc(control_domain) AS domains_assessed values(eval(if(status NOT IN ("pass", "compliant", "effective"), control_id, null()))) AS failing_controls BY control_domain
| eval effectiveness_pct=round(100*effective_controls/total_controls, 1)
| where effectiveness_pct<100
| sort effectiveness_pct
| table control_domain, total_controls, effective_controls, effectiveness_pct, failing_controls
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
